page: Microsoft Azure F32-16ams_v7 is a memory-intensive virtual machine belonging to the Famsv7 server family. It is powered by an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture. The server features 16 dedicated physical cores and 16 vCPUs running at a clock speed of 4.5 GHz, with 1.0 thread per core. It is configured with 256.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high resource density of 16.0 GB of RAM per core. There is no local storage bundled with this instance, and it includes zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data reveals distinct performance characteristics. The single-core CPU performance is highly competitive, with stress-ng single-core testing and single-threaded gzip and bzip3 compression operations placing in the top 10% tier. Multi-threaded decompression is also strong, ranking in the top 25%. However, multi-core CPU performance and Passmark CPU scores fall within the average range. Memory bandwidth benchmarks, including both small-block cached and large-block uncached read and write tests, consistently register in the average tier, though the Passmark memory score is strong.

For specialized workloads, the server demonstrates strong prompt processing and top-tier text generation speeds during LLM inference with small models, though performance scales down to average levels for medium and large models. Given the high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 1:16 and the lack of local storage, this server is designed for memory-intensive workloads that rely on network-attached premium storage. It is suitable for database operations, memory-cached applications, and memory-bound analytical tasks rather than local storage-heavy or highly parallelized multi-core compute tasks.
description: Microsoft Azure F32-16ams_v7 is an x86_64 memory-intensive virtual machine from the Famsv7 family, powered by a 16-core AMD EPYC 9V45 processor running at 4.5 GHz with 16 vCPUs and 256.0 GB of RAM. This configuration provides a high memory-to-core ratio of 16.0 GB per core. The server lacks local storage and complimentary public IPv4 addresses, relying on network-attached storage. In benchmark testing, the instance exhibits top-tier single-core CPU performance and single-threaded compression speeds, alongside strong multi-threaded decompression and small-model LLM text generation. However, multi-core CPU performance and memory bandwidth benchmarks remain in the average tier. This server is optimized for memory-bound workloads, database operations, and applications requiring high single-core efficiency paired with substantial memory capacity.
og_description: Microsoft Azure F32-16ams_v7 is a memory-intensive virtual machine featuring 16 AMD EPYC cores, 256 GB of RAM, and top-tier single-core performance, designed for memory-bound workloads without local storage.

256.0 GB of system memory providing 16.0 GB of RAM per core
